commits.body:
Surface the AI's tool calls in the chat panel as collapsible
cards with tool name and raw output (matching the handling of reasoning blocks).
Gated behind ENABLE_AI_TOOL_CALLS_DISPLAY (off).

Also refactors AiChatContent tests to extract shared setup helpers to please CodeScene.

ETC-726.title: Negotiate supported client tools between plugin and middleware
ETC-726.description:
Currently, the middleware advertises a fixed list of client tools to OpenAI regardless of which tools the plugin version in the user's browser actually supports. 

When OpenAI calls an unsupported tool, it silently fails and the AI features in Etch appear **broken**.

This change adds capability negotiation: the plugin sends its supported tool names with every initial request, and the middleware only exposes the intersection to OpenAI. Old plugin versions that don't send the field fall back to a known-safe default set.

**Acceptance Criteria**

- [X] If I'm using an old version of the plugin (1.4.15 — meaning, `supported_client_tools` is not declared), the AI provider receives a hardcoded “classic tools” set: `rag_retrieve`, `docsWebSearch`, `list_post_types`, `create_etch_loop`.
- [X] When I send a message to AI, only client tools the plugin declares support for (in `supported_client_tools`) are offered to the AI provider
  - [X] Tools only present on the plugin side are dropped in the negotiation
  - [X] Tools only present on the server side are dropped in the negotiation
- [X] When the plugin declares an empty set of supported tools (`supported_client_tools = []`), the middleware doesn't accept it and returns an error to the plugin.
  (error code: `EMPTY_SUPPORTED_TOOLS`)
- [X] If the plugin sends a malformed `supported_client_tools`, the middleware doesn't accept it and returns an error to the plugin.
  (error code: `MALFORMED_SUPPORTED_TOOLS`)
- [X] If the plugin sends only unsupported tools (`available_tools = []` after intersection):
  * The middleware blocks the request and returns an error to the plugin 
    (error code `NO_TOOLS_INTERSECTION`)
- [X] If the provider sends a call for a non-declared tool:
  - [X] The middleware blocks the request and returns an error back to the provider, indicating the tool is not supported by this client.
  - [X] The plugin blocks the tool call on the way back, and creates an error 
    (error code `UNDECLARED_TOOL_CALLED`)
  - [X] We display an error toast to the user:
    “There was an issue with this request (UNDECLARED_TOOL_CALLED). Please contact support.”
  - [X] The plugin logs the failure to the Etch activity log
- [ ] ~~Other requests in the same “user turn” (~~`~~continue~~`~~ requests) are unaffected~~

**Implementation notes**

* When a new tool handler is introduced on the plugin side, it is automatically included in the negotiation — no other manual change required.

5 touch points across both repos according to Claude (this could be outdated, keeping it in case it's still partly useful):

1. `aiClientTools.ts` (etch) — export the handler key list (already `Object.keys(handlers)`, just needs exporting)
2. `AiChat.svelte.ts` (etch) — add `supported_client_tools` to the initial request payload; omit on `continue`
3. `AiRoutes.php` (etch) — verify `supported_client_tools` is not stripped by body sanitization
4. `schema.ts` (etch-middleware) — add `supported_client_tools?: string[]` as optional to `InitialRequestSchema`
5. `service.ts` (etch-middleware) — filter client tools against `supported_client_tools` when present; absent → default set; empty → no client tools; server tools always pass through

ETC-747.title: Thousands of Page Errors and maybe relatet 404
ETC-747.description:
The backstory of this is a problem I had with a new hosting provider.

After working on an etch site, I was blocked by the firewall. They told me it was because the 404 page was hit too often.

I then suspected it was because of something going on in the system and I checked the console. There I see thousands of quickly accumulating "page errors". It says : "an @property rule was ignored". When I managed to click on the link in the error (always different line references), it told me "Content unavailable. Resource was not cached".

[https://share.netzmaedchen.de/4guqWKjB](<https://share.netzmaedchen.de/4guqWKjB>)

I don't know if those are the cause of the blocking, but still, it might be worth a look to get rid of the errors piling up. I have this on ALL my etch installs, also deactivated all other plugins. Still happening.

## Thanks for looking into this. 
Regards
Anja

### Steps to reproduce

Open Etch, go into Console and check (see screenshot)

---

### Environment

Plugin Version:  1.4.16

Theme Version: 0.0.7

WordPress Version: 6.9.4

Machine: Mac

Browser: Chromium

---

### Magic Login

[https://etchplaya.instawp.co/wp-admin/?wtlwp_token=57655094233ed0cfc94dbde870016af30bbd7f9cbabcc7e49b65b91aa5cbf3621a18c070e289d56a17060acca44658bab5ef2cb7527e61851ebce89c66e81f7b](<https://etchplaya.instawp.co/wp-admin/?wtlwp_token=57655094233ed0cfc94dbde870016af30bbd7f9cbabcc7e49b65b91aa5cbf3621a18c070e289d56a17060acca44658bab5ef2cb7527e61851ebce89c66e81f7b>)

ETC-745.title: Properties panel "select" type gets buried
ETC-745.description:
Sometimes when engaging with the property to select a different option, the drop-down items get buried outside of the viewable area.

The video describes it best:

## [https://jam.dev/c/e40010b9-b99b-40b1-baa5-44a6a2fcb44b](<https://jam.dev/c/e40010b9-b99b-40b1-baa5-44a6a2fcb44b>)

### Steps to reproduce

Click on a component that has a select type property, that is also positioned near the bottom of the viewable screen.

Then engage with some other properties, come back to it, and you'll see that it gets buried.

---

### Environment

Plugin Version: 1.4.16

Theme Version: 0.0.7

WordPress Version: 6.9.4

Machine: PC

Browser: Chromium

ETC-746.title: ENABLE_CONDITION_PROP Error
ETC-746.description:
## When building a component in Etch, adding a condition prop now throws an error in Gutenberg, removing the ability to manage the component. It started in 1.4.16.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a component with a condition prop. Edit in Gutenberg and click on the component, and the block will present an error "This block has encountered an error and cannot be previewed." Viewing the console, there is an error

react-dom.min.js?ver=18.3.1.1:121 FeatureFlagError: Feature flag "ENABLE_CONDITION_PROP" is not defined
    at Fc (index.js?ver=3381f81…56e735a2ac:1:261872)
    at Ud.property (index.js?ver=3381f81…56e735a2ac:1:295046)
    at index.js?ver=3381f81…56e735a2ac:1:300164
    at Vd (index.js?ver=3381f81…56e735a2ac:1:300188)
    at mf (react-dom.min.js?ver=18.3.1.1:105:412)
    at Qk (react-dom.min.js?ver=18.3.1.1:250:214)
    at Ri (react-dom.min.js?ver=18.3.1.1:195:185)
    at Ok (react-dom.min.js?ver=18.3.1.1:195:116)
    at Nd (react-dom.min.js?ver=18.3.1.1:194:492)
    at Qf (react-dom.min.js?ver=18.3.1.1:187:439)

ETC-724.title: Allow `create_etch_loop` to create loops for any post type
ETC-724.description:
Currently, the `create_etch_loop` tool hardcodes `post_type` to `"post"` (`createEtchLoop.ts:13`), preventing the AI from creating loop definitions that query custom post types. 

This change loosens the schema to accept any post type slug, aligned with the same explicitness model applied to `create_posts` in [ETC-723](https://linear.app/digital-gravy/issue/ETC-723/allow-create-posts-to-create-posts-of-any-post-type).

**Acceptance Criteria**

- [ ] When the AI is in Ask mode, it is not aware of the tool and cannot call it (no regression).
- [ ] When `create_etch_loop` is called without a `post_type`, it returns an `INVALID_ARGS` error with a specific, explicit error message to the AI model (so it can self-correct).
- [ ] When `create_etch_loop` is called with a `post_type` value of any string (e.g. `page`, `project`), the loop definition is created with that post type.

**Implementation notes**

1. **Schema** (`createEtchLoop.ts:13`) — change `post_type: z.literal('post')` to `post_type: z.string()`
2. **Tests** (`createEtchLoop.test.ts:52`) — `'does not create loop for wp-query with unsupported post type'` needs to be inverted: `post_type: 'service'` should now succeed, not fail; repurpose as a success case
3. The MANDATORY `list_post_types` prerequisite is already in the tool description — no change needed there
